prompt.task: Extract structured information from the receipt text. Handle OCR errors, missing fields, and inconsistent formatting as shown in examples.
prompt.context:
The input is OCR text from photographed receipts. OCR often produces: - Misspellings and character substitutions (0 for O, l for 1) - Missing or corrupted text - Inconsistent date/time formats - Partial or cut-off information
The output will be used for expense categorization and reporting.

constraints.constraint: Dates must be normalized to ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD)
Currency must be extracted and normalized (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.)
Use null for fields that cannot be reliably extracted
Use "uncertain" confidence when making reasonable inferences from corrupted text
Never invent information that isn't in the source

example.description: Clean receipt with all fields present
example.input:
STARBUCKS STORE #4521 1234 MAIN ST SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94102
02/15/2024 10:32 AM
GRANDE LATTE 5.75 BLUEBERRY MUFFIN 3.45 ------------------------ SUBTOTAL 9.20 TAX 0.83 ------------------------ TOTAL 10.03
VISA **** 4532

example.description: Receipt with OCR errors and missing fields
example.input:
WAL*MART #2847 BENTONV1LLE AR
O1/O8/24
GROCERIES $42.17 CLEANING SUPP $8.99
T0TAL $51.16
DEBIT XXXX
example.annotation: OCR errors handled: "BENTONV1LLE" → "Bentonville", "O1/O8/24" → "2024-01-08", "T0TAL" → recognized as total. Date confidence is "uncertain" because year was inferred (could be 2024 or 2023). Last four digits missing so null.

example.description: Foreign currency receipt with different format
example.input:
TESCO EXPRESS LONDON W1K 4QL
15-MAR-2024 18:47
MEAL DEAL £3.50 WATER 500ML £0.80 CRISPS £1.20 ------------------ VAT £0.92 TOTAL £5.50
CONTACTLESS
example.annotation: Currency detected from £ symbol. Date format DD-MMM-YYYY normalized to ISO 8601. VAT instead of "tax" - include tax_label for clarity. Subtotal calculated (total - tax) since not explicitly shown.

When data is significantly corrupted, use "uncertain" or "low" confidence. "2/1" could be Feb 1, Jan 2, or part of something else - use null. Add extraction_notes when quality is poor to flag for human review.
Never invent addresses, split amounts evenly, or guess missing data. Item amounts are unknown - use null, not fabricated splits. Only the total is actually visible in the receipt.

prompt.input:
UBER TRIP Mar 23 2O24
TRIP FARE $18.45 BOOKING FEE $2.50 SURGE $4.30 ----------- SUBTOT $25.25 TOTAL $25.25
CHG VISA ...8821
PICKUP: 345 MARKET ST DROP: SFO TERMINAL 2
prompt.output_format: Return a single JSON object matching the structure in the examples. Include all fields even if null. No markdown fences.

checks.check: No fabricated data - null for unknown fields
